name: Cell The Unit of Life
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

tests.testPaperName: Cell The Unit Of Life Test-01
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

questions.question: New cells generate from:
questions.question_raw: New cells generate from:
questions.explanation: All living organisms are composed of cells and products of cells.all cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.question_raw: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.explanation: The first man to witness a live cell under a microscope was Anton van Leeuwenhoek, who in 1674 described the algae Spirogyra. Van Leeuwenhoek probably also saw bacteria.

questions.question: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.question_raw: Cytoskeleton is made of.
questions.explanation: Cytoskeleton is a series of intercellular proteins that help a cell with shape, support andmovement. So, cytoskeleton is made up of proteinaceous filaments.

questions.question: Select the incorrect pairing:
questions.question_raw: Select the incorrect pairing:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria contain cristae, finger-like projection of inner layer, thylakoid is present in chloroplast and lysosome is a digestive enzyme but RNA is absent in Chromosome.

questions.question: Which is common in plant and animal cells?
questions.question_raw: Which is common in plant and animal cells?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are present in both plants as well as animal cells. Plastids and central vacuoles are present in plant cells and centrioles are present in only animal cells.

questions.question: Animal cell does not possess:
questions.question_raw: Animal cell does not possess:
questions.explanation: An animal cell does not possess plasmodesmata but they contain ribosomes, centriole, and plasmalemma. Plasmodesmata are the connection between two plant cells.

questions.question: The ability of a living somatic nucleated cell to form the complete organism is called:
questions.question_raw: The ability of a living somatic nucleated cell to form the complete organism is called:
questions.explanation: Cellular Totipotency is the ability of a single cell to produce all cell types and to organize them into an entire organism when cultured in a suitable culture medium at the appropriate temperature and aeration conditions. Spores and Zygote are examples of totipotent cells.

questions.question: The most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane is:
questions.question_raw: The most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane is:
questions.explanation: Fluid-Mosaic model is the most recent and accepted model of the plasma membrane. This model was proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. The quasi-fluid nature of lipid enables the lateral movement of proteins within the overall bilayer.

questions.question: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.question_raw: Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called:
questions.explanation: All animal cell has two small organelles called centrioles. Each centriole is surrounded by dense amorphous protoplasmic masses called Massules.

questions.question: The photograph or diagram of metaphase chromosome arranged in homologous pair according to their length and thickness, the position of the centromere is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The photograph or diagram of metaphase chromosome arranged in homologous pair according to their length and thickness, the position of the centromere is called ________.
questions.explanation: Idiogram is a diagrammatic or systematized representation of the chromosome complement of an individual, species, or another group of organisms, indicating the number of chromosomes and their lengths, arm length ratio, centromere position, banding pattern.

questions.question: The membrane complex formed in the folding of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The membrane complex formed in the folding of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells is called ________.
questions.explanation: A special membranous structure is a mesosome which is formed by the extensions of the plasma membrane into the cell.

questions.question: The smallest free-living organism is______.
questions.question_raw: The smallest free-living organism is______.
questions.explanation: PPLO (Pleuro Pneumonia Like Organisms). They are generally smaller and multiply more rapidly than the eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: In certain bacteria, the glycocalyx is formed into loose sheath layer is called as ________.
questions.question_raw: In certain bacteria, the glycocalyx is formed into loose sheath layer is called as ________.
questions.explanation: Glycocalyx differs in composition and thickness among different bacteria. It could be a loose sheath called the slime layer in some, while in others it may be thick and tough called the capsule. The cell wall determines the shape of the cell and provides strong structural support.

questions.question: The bacterial flagellum consists of
questions.question_raw: The bacterial flagellum consists of
questions.explanation:
A bacterial flagellum has 3 basic parts: a filament, a hook, and a basal body.
The filament is the rigid, helical structure that extends from the cell surface.
The hook is a flexible coupling between the filament and the basal body.
The basal body consists of a rod and a series of rings that anchor the flagellum to the cell wall and the cytoplasmic membrane.

questions.question: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.question_raw: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.explanation: The shapes of bacteria are bacillus (rod-like), coccus (spherical), vibrio (comma-shaped) and spirillum (spiral).

tests.testPaperName: Cell The Unit Of Life Test-02
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

questions.question: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.question_raw: The rod-shaped bacteria is called as_______.
questions.explanation: The shapes of bacteria are bacillus (rod-like), coccus (spherical), vibrio (comma-shaped) and spirillum (spiral).

questions.question: In prokaryotes, the reserved food material is stored in:
questions.question_raw: In prokaryotes, the reserved food material is stored in:
questions.explanation: Reserve material in prokaryotic cells is stored in the cytoplasm in the form of inclusion bodies.

questions.question: Tubulin occurs in:
questions.question_raw: Tubulin occurs in:
questions.explanation: Tubulin is a kind of protein that is present in the cilia and flagella of cells having it. It provides flexibility to the cilia and flagella.

questions.question: Arrangement of ciliary microtubules is:
questions.question_raw: Arrangement of ciliary microtubules is:
questions.explanation: Cilia present in prokaryotic organisms consist of microtubules. These microtubules are arranged in 9+2 order in which 2 forms the centre and 9 are arranged at the periphery.

questions.question: Which does not occur in cell membrane?
questions.question_raw: Which does not occur in cell membrane?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is made up of lipid bilayer consisting of different components like phospholipids, glycolipids, and cholesterol. However, proline is an alpha-amino acid used in protein synthesis and is a major constituent of collagen.

questions.question: Lipid molecules of plasma membrane occur
questions.question_raw: Lipid molecules of plasma membrane occur
questions.explanation: Phospholipids found in the plasma membrane is a lipid bilayer having a hydrophilic region called the head and a hydrophobic region called the tail. The hydrophilic region can interact with water due to the presence of a phosphate group which is polar, like water. The membrane is in contact with water on both the inner and outer sides. The hydrophilic heads arrange automatically on the outer side of the bilayer due to polarity and hydrophobic heads are facing each other between the bilayer, thus arranging all lipid molecules parallel to each other.

questions.question: The plasma membrane consists mainly of:
questions.question_raw: The plasma membrane consists mainly of:

options.label: A
options.text: Proteins embedded in a carbohydrate bilayer

options.label: B
options.text: Proteins embedded in a phospholipids bilayer

options.label: D
options.text: Proteins embedded in a polymer of glucose molecules

correct_option_texts: Proteins embedded in a phospholipids bilayer
questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is composed of lipids that are arranged in a bilayer. Also, the lipids are arranged within the membrane with the polar head towards the outer sides and the hydrophobic tails towards the inner part. The membrane proteins can be classified as integral or peripheral. Peripheral proteins lie on the surface.

questions.question: The nucleus was first described by:
questions.question_raw: The nucleus was first described by:
questions.explanation: Nucleus as a cell organelle was first described by Robert Brown as early as 1831.

questions.question: Lysosomes are produced by:
questions.question_raw: Lysosomes are produced by:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are manufactured and budded into the cytoplasm by the Golgi apparatus with enzymes inside. The enzymes that are within the lysosome are made in the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which is then delivered to the Golgi apparatus via transport vesicles.

questions.question: The eukaryotic ribosomes are of_____.
questions.question_raw: The eukaryotic ribosomes are of_____.
questions.explanation: The eukaryotic ribosomes are the 80S while the prokaryotic ribosomes are 70S. Here ‘S’ stands for the sedimentation coefficient; it indirectly is a measure of density and size.

questions.question: The vacuoles are bound by the single membrane is called as_________.
questions.question_raw: The vacuoles are bound by the single membrane is called as_________.
questions.explanation: The vacuole is bound by a single membrane called tonoplast. In-plant cells the vacuoles can occupy up to 90% of the volume of the cell. In plants, the tonoplast facilitates the transport of a number of ions.

questions.question: The chromosome having centromeres at one terminal end is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The chromosome having centromeres at one terminal end is called ________.
questions.explanation: A telocentric chromosome's centromere is located at the terminal end of the chromosome. Telomeres may extend from both ends of the chromosome their shape is like I during anaphase. For example, the standard house mouse karyotype has only telocentric chromosomes. Humans do not possess telocentric chromosomes.

questions.question: Nucleoplasm otherwise called as ______.
questions.question_raw: Nucleoplasm otherwise called as ______.
questions.explanation: The nuclear matrix or the nucleoplasm contains nucleolus and chromatin. The nucleoli are spherical structures present in the nucleoplasm.

questions.question: Lysosomes are rich in hydrolytic enzymes such as:
questions.question_raw: Lysosomes are rich in hydrolytic enzymes such as:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are found to be very rich in almost all types of hydrolytic enzymes (hydrolases - lipases, proteases, carbohydrases) optimally active at the acidic pH. These enzymes are capable of digesting carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids.

questions.question: Mitochondria is called Powerhouse of the cell because:
questions.question_raw: Mitochondria is called Powerhouse of the cell because:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are called as powerhouse of the cell because it contains an enzyme for cellular respiration in which energy is released in the form of ATP.

tests.testPaperName: Cell The Unit Of Life Test-03
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

questions.question: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are double membranous structure. The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called cristae.

questions.question: The staining procedure is developed by:
questions.question_raw: The staining procedure is developed by:
questions.explanation: The staining procedure developed by Gram viz., those that take up the gram stain are gram-positive and the others that do not are called gram-negative bacteria.

questions.question: SER differ from RER in:
questions.question_raw: SER differ from RER in:
questions.explanation: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) differs from the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) in absence of ribosome on the surface of the endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Golgi apparatus is the site for the formation of:
questions.question_raw: Golgi apparatus is the site for the formation of:
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is the important site of formation of glycoproteins and glycolipids.

questions.question: Elaioplasts belongs to:
questions.question_raw: Elaioplasts belongs to:
questions.explanation:
The leucoplasts are the colourless plastids of varied shapes and sizes with stored nutrients. Leucoplast includes:
 Amyloplasts storing carbohydrates.
 Elaioplasts storing oils and fats.
 Aleuroplasts storing proteins.

questions.question: How much percent of vacuoles occupy the volume of the cell in plant cells?
questions.question_raw: How much percent of vacuoles occupy the volume of the cell in plant cells?
questions.explanation: In a mature cell, the vacuole may occupy as much as 90 percent of the cell volume, such that the rest of the cell contents are flattened against the cell membrane.

questions.question: Chlorophyll is located inside_______.
questions.question_raw: Chlorophyll is located inside_______.
questions.explanation: The chloroplast consists of a number of organized flattened membranous sacs called the thylakoids, Chlorophyll pigments are present in the thylakoids.

questions.question: Which one is present nearest to plasmamembrane?
questions.question_raw: Which one is present nearest to plasmamembrane?
questions.explanation: The cell wall of a young plant cell, the primary walls capable of growth, which gradually diminishes as the cell matures and the secondary wall is formed on the inner (towards membrane) side of the cell.

questions.question: The cytoskeleton is made of:
questions.question_raw: The cytoskeleton is made of:
questions.explanation: An elaborate network of filamentous proteinaceous structures present in the cytoplasm is collectively referred to as the cytoskeleton.

questions.question: The movement of ions against the concentration gradient is
questions.question_raw: The movement of ions against the concentration gradient is
questions.explanation: Active transport is the movement of molecules across a cell membrane in the direction against their concentration gradient, i.e. moving from an area of lower concentration to an area of higher concentration.

questions.question: The chromatin contains some basic proteins are called as ________.
questions.question_raw: The chromatin contains some basic proteins are called as ________.
questions.explanation: Histone proteins the basic packer and arranger of chromatin.

questions.question: The centrioles are absent in______.
questions.question_raw: The centrioles are absent in______.
questions.explanation: Animal cells have centrioles which are absent in almost all plant cells.

questions.question: The ribosomes of a polysome translate:
questions.question_raw: The ribosomes of a polysome translate:
questions.explanation: Several ribosomes may attach to a single mRNA and form a chain called polyribosomes or polysome. The ribosomes of a polysome translate the mRNA into proteins.

questions.question: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.question_raw: Middle lamella is composed of:
questions.explanation: The middle lamella is a layer mainly of calcium pectate which holds or glues the different neighbouring cells together.

questions.question: Plasmodesmata take parts in:
questions.question_raw: Plasmodesmata take parts in:
questions.explanation: The plasmodesmata consist of pores, or channels, lying between individual plant cells, and connect the symplastic space in the plant. They can also be termed as "bridges" between two plant cells. The plasmodesmata separate the outer cell membranes of the plant cells. A plasmodesma (plural plasmodesmata, singular plasmodesma) is a channel through the cell wall that allows molecules and substances to move back and forth as needed.

tests.testPaperName: Cell The Unit Of Life Test-04
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

questions.question: Who was the first to explain that cells divide?
questions.question_raw: Who was the first to explain that cells divide?
questions.explanation: The German doctor Rudolf Virchow proposed in 1855 that all cells result from the division of previously existing cells, and this idea became a key piece of modern cell theory.

questions.question: Which of the following statements with reference to the cytoskeleton is not correct?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements with reference to the cytoskeleton is not correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Cytoskeleton maintains the shape of the cell

options.label: B
options.text: Cytoskeleton helpful in production of ATP

correct_option_texts: Cytoskeleton helpful in production of ATP
questions.explanation: The cytoskeleton in a cell is involved in functions like mechanical support, motility, maintenance of the shape of the cell.

questions.question: The organelle involved in respiration is_______.
questions.question_raw: The organelle involved in respiration is_______.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the sites of aerobic respiration. They produce cellular energy in the form of ATP.

questions.question: Cell organelles with a single membrane are ________.
questions.question_raw: Cell organelles with a single membrane are ________.
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are single membrane structures containing enzymes for digestion of all types of macromolecules.

questions.question: The site for the light reactions is ________.
questions.question_raw: The site for the light reactions is ________.
questions.explanation: In-plant cells, the chloroplasts are responsible for trapping light energy essential for photosynthesis. The grana, in the plastid, is the site of light reactions.

questions.question: Select the incorrect matching.
questions.question_raw: Select the incorrect matching.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains the chromosomes which in turn contain the genetic material, DNA.

questions.question: The stacks of closely packed thylakoids are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The stacks of closely packed thylakoids are called ________.
questions.explanation: Granum: A stacked membranous structure within the chloroplasts of plants and green algae that contains chlorophyll and is the site of the light reactions of photosynthesis. The saclike membranes that makeup grana are known as thylakoids.

questions.question: Which one of the following is common between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cell?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is common between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cell?
questions.explanation: Most prokaryotic cells, particularly the bacterial cells, have a chemically complex cell envelope. All eukaryotic cells are not identical. Plant and animal cells are different as the former possess cell walls.

questions.question: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.question_raw: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model explains various observations regarding the structure of functional cell membranes. The model, which was devised by SJ Singer and GL Nicolson in 1972, describes the cell membrane as a two-dimensional liquid that restricts the lateral diffusion of membrane components.

questions.question: Proteins synthesized by ribosomes on the endoplasmic reticulum are modified in________.
questions.question_raw: Proteins synthesized by ribosomes on the endoplasmic reticulum are modified in________.
questions.explanation: The cisternae of the Golgi apparatus is responsible for transporting, modifying, and packaging proteins and lipids into vesicles for delivery to targeted destinations.

questions.question: Subunits of 80S ribosome are:
questions.question_raw: Subunits of 80S ribosome are:
questions.explanation:
Each ribosome consists of two subunits.
Eukaryotes have 80S ribosomes, each consisting of a small (40S) and large (60S) subunit.
Their 40S subunit has an 18S RNA (1900 nucleotides) and 33 proteins.
The large subunit is composed of a 5S RNA (120 nucleotides), 28S RNA (4700 nucleotides), a 5.8S RNA (160 nucleotides) subunits, and 46 proteins.

questions.question: Cell drinking is another name of _______.
questions.question_raw: Cell drinking is another name of _______.
questions.explanation: Cell drinking is another name of pinocytosis. Pinocytosis, a process by which liquid droplets are ingested by living cells. Pinocytosis is one type of endocytosis, the general process by which cells engulf external substances, gathering them into special membrane-bound vesicles contained within the cell.

questions.question: Which one is not a part of the Golgi complex?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a part of the Golgi complex?
questions.explanation: Golgi complex or Golgi body consists of cisternae, tubules and spherical vesicles. Glycosome is not present in the Golgi complex.

questions.question: Lysosomes are called suicide bag because it contain:
questions.question_raw: Lysosomes are called suicide bag because it contain:
questions.explanation: Lysosome is called a suicide bag because it contain a large number of digestive enzyme that can digest the whole cell in case of rapture.

questions.question: Which is not a function of the vacuole in a plant cell?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of the vacuole in a plant cell?
questions.explanation: Plant cell contains large central vacuole which helps in waste disposal, cell elongation and storage but not involved in the formation of H_2O_2.

tests.testPaperName: Cell The Unit Of Life Test-05
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Cell The Unit of Life

questions.question: The ribosome is granules formed of:
questions.question_raw: The ribosome is granules formed of:
questions.explanation: A ribosome is a small, dense granular particle comprising usually three or four ribosomal RNA molecules and more than 50 protein molecules, interconnected to form the site of protein synthesis.

questions.question: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.question_raw: A clear zone around Golgi apparatus is zone of _______.
questions.explanation: The clear zone surrounding the Golgi apparatus is a zone of exclusion, where no other cellular activity takes place.

questions.question: Largest organelles in the cell are:
questions.question_raw: Largest organelles in the cell are:

options.label: C
options.text: Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell

options.label: D
options.text: Centrioles in animals and plastids in plant cell

correct_option_texts: Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is the largest cell organelles in animal cell and chloroplast is the largest cell organelles in a plant cell.

questions.question: A cell membrane shows fluid behaviour due to:
questions.question_raw: A cell membrane shows fluid behaviour due to:

options.label: B
options.text: Sandwich of protein between lipids layer

questions.explanation: A cell membrane shows fluid behaviour due to the mosaic of lipid and proteins. Fluid mosaic model of the plasma membrane is the most accepted and recent model.

questions.question: The structural element of chromatin is _________.
questions.question_raw: The structural element of chromatin is _________.
questions.explanation: Chromatin is the thread-like structure present in the nucleus. Chromatin has beads like structure called nucleosome that contain histone protein and DNA.

questions.question: Two cell organelles in plant cell having their own genetic material are called ________.
questions.question_raw: Two cell organelles in plant cell having their own genetic material are called ________.
questions.explanation: Genetic material is mainly present in the nucleus of the cell but these two cell organelles also contain their own genetic material.

questions.question: Longest cell in human body is ________.
questions.question_raw: Longest cell in human body is ________.
questions.explanation: A nerve cell or neuron is the longest cell in the human body. They are responsible for the transfer of information from one part of the body to the other in form of nerve impulses.

questions.question: Smallest free-living organism are:
questions.question_raw: Smallest free-living organism are:
questions.explanation: Pleuro Pneumonia Like Organism (PPLO) or mycoplasma is considered as the smallest free-living organism.

questions.question: The fluid-filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from the cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fluid-filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from the cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane called ________.
questions.explanation: Tonoplasts are the fluid filled vacuoles or vesicles which are separated from cytoplasm by a selectively permeable membrane. It helps in maintaining turgidity of the cell.

questions.question: Which one is present nearest to plasma membrane?
questions.question_raw: Which one is present nearest to plasma membrane?
questions.explanation: Secondary wall is formed inside the primary wall. Secondary wall is present nearest to the plasma membrane of a plant cell.

questions.question: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is______.
questions.question_raw: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is______.
questions.explanation: Ions connected with forming cross-bridge is calcium ions (Ca^2+) that help the impulse to cross the gaps at the synapse.

questions.question: The joined area between adjacent cells are ________.
questions.question_raw: The joined area between adjacent cells are ________.
questions.explanation: A desmosome is a cell structure specialised for the cell to cell adhesion. They are located on lateral sides of plasma membranes.

questions.question: Which one is not a difference between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a difference between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic cells and differentiated on the basis of the number of chromosomes, presence or absence of nuclear membrane and presence and absence of membrane-bounded organelles. The cell wall is not a difference.

questions.question: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called ________.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are double membranous structure. The inner membrane of mitochondria thrown into folds to form finger-like structure is called cristae.

questions.question: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.question_raw: Who first saw and described a live cell?
questions.explanation: The first man to witness a live cell under a microscope was Anton van Leeuwenhoek, who in 1674 described the algae Spirogyra. Van Leeuwenhoek probably also saw bacteria.
